calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-19-2017 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-09-2017 Continued To Aug-09-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-21-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Naalie Orr, Deputy City Attorney (415-554-3849) of Office of the City Attorney, City and County of San Francisco for Defendant.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY & COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend as to both causes of action. Plaintiff's first cause of action for tax refund fails to state a cognizable claim. The court takes judicial notice of the grant deed recorded on April 29, 2016. A condominium consists of a separate interest coupled with an interest in the common area. (County of Los Angeles v. Hartford Acc. & Indem.Co. (1970) 3 Cal.App.3d 809, 814). The grant deed shows that plaintiff conveyed an undivided interest in the property to the buyers. In addition, the deed included a restrictive covenant that prevented the sale of separate condominiums without plaintiff's prior written consent. The grant deed shows that the City properly taxed plaintiff. Plaintiff's second cause of action for promissory estoppel also fails to state a cognizable claim. The second cause of action runs afoul of the rule that the government cannot be estopped from collecting a proper tax. (Rickley v. County of Los Angeles (2004) 114 Cal.App.4th 1002, 1013.) Since it appears that plaintiff is unable to remedy the deficiencies in its causes of action, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. Order is signed. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. =(302/HK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-06-2017 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Natalie M. Orr, of Office of the City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3800, for defendants;
No other appearances.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY & COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO (CCSF), CCSF ASSESSOR-RECORDER, CCSF TAX COLLECTOR's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT as to sole cause of action is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. The City's request for judicial notice is granted and the court takes judicial notice of the grant deed recorded on April 29, 2016. A condominium is the conveyance of a separate interest coupled with an interest in the common area. (County of Los Angeles v. Hartford Acc. & Indem.Co. (1970) 3 Cal.App.3d 809, 814; City of West Hollywood v. Beverly Towers, Inc. (1991) 52 Cal.3d 1184, 1190.) The grant deed shows that plaintiff SST Investments, LLC conveyed undivided interests in the property to the buyers. The deed also included a restrictive covenant that prevented the sale of separate condominiums without plaintiff's prior written consent. The face of the grant deed shows that the City properly taxed plaintiff. However, paragraphs 22-29 of the complaint allege facts showing that might support the application of equitable estoppel principle based on the City's conduct. If it can do so in good faith, plaintiff is granted leave to allege specific facts that showing estoppel or any other basis for the relief it seeks. Revenue & Taxation Code 2188.6 does not apply to this case because that provision discusses the assessor's discretion in assessing property taxes and this action involves the conduct of the recorder, a different government officer. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Kelley DeYoung, CSR No. 6780, email: kelleycsr@aol.com; Dept. 302.
